assumptions: The intragroup dividend has given rise to a recognised receivable/payable that is a monetary item.
The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities, which is relevant when assessing a recognised intragroup monetary receivable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a separate top-level model, including for highly probable forecast transactions and exposures whose cash flows are affected by foreign exchange risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model for foreign exchange exposures in consolidated financial statements.
